description: This figure presents a comprehensive quantitative decomposition of strengthening mechanisms in Fe-Al based ferritic low-density steels, specifically illustrating the calculated contributions to yield strength for Fe-6.8Al alloy across different thermomechanical processing conditions. The chart compares three material states: 98% cold-rolled (CR), and isothermally annealed at 750°C for 5 min and 60 min. It displays the individual contributions from solid solution strengthening (~95.6 MPa, constant), lattice frictional stress (41 MPa), order strengthening from nano-sized Fe3Al D03 ordered phases (2.1–3.0 nm diameter, volume fraction varying with Al content), Hall-Petch grain boundary strengthening (varying with grain size from 25.2 ± 5.0 μm to 48.3 ± 1.2 μm), and Taylor dislocation strengthening (dominant in CR state with density ~2.2 × 10^15 m^-2, negligible after recrystallization). The experimental validation shows predicted yield strengths matching measured values of 1800 MPa (CR), 700 MPa (5 min), and 265 MPa (60 min), demonstrating the transition from dislocation-based to grain-boundary and ordered-phase controlled strengthening mechanisms during recrystallization and grain growth.
